element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns steering and aim before live delivery route
Primary objective; 10 of 20 houses are subscribers at start
Bonus points for property damage; optional destructive play
Collision causes life loss; requires evasive steering
Resource management; must restock limited paper supply mid-route
Unlimited papers; targets and ramps for skill-based bonus points
Perfect deliveries add subscribers; damage/misses cancel subscriptions
Sunday papers are heavier, throw slower; increased obstacle density

typical_relief_triggers: Entering training course (safe zone, unlimited papers)
Picking up paper bundle to restore ammo
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Rhythmic stop-and-go: tension builds through obstacle-laden route delivery, releases during training course bonus phase; cycle repeats daily with increasing difficulty

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Non-subscriber houses marked with duller colors; optional destruction for bonus points signals alternative play style
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Press B while throwing newspaper (Japanese version memo); effect unclear but suggests hidden bonus mechanic

difficulty_curve.early_game: Monday-Tuesday: sparse obstacles, manageable subscriber count (10 houses), high paper availability; player learns core rhythm
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wednesday-Friday: increased obstacle density, traffic patterns become unpredictable, subscriber loss risk rises; mastery of steering required
difficulty_curve.late_game: Saturday-Sunday: maximum obstacles, heavier papers reduce throw speed/distance, minimal margin for error; endurance test

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level (Monday-Sunday) consists of two phases: dangerous delivery route (tension) followed by safe training course with targets/ramps (relief). Training course provides skill expression and bonus economy separate from delivery stress.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Monday (tutorial difficulty establishes pattern for all 7 days)

notable_level_patterns.description: Subscriber count caps at 10 and can only decrease or increase by 1 per level. This creates a feedback loop: early perfection adds subscribers, increasing future route complexity; failures cascade into subscription loss and game-over condition.
Only Sunday introduces mechanical change (heavier papers = slower throws). This single late-game twist recontextualizes all prior skill mastery, forcing timing adjustment in final route.

Isometric-style view creates depth ambiguity; steering must account for forward/backward and left/right simultaneously. Spatial grammar reinforces that collision avoidance requires 2D thinking, not purely lateral dodge patterns.
